Optimizely.summary_abuse: Optimizely has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise A/B testing and experimentation platform used by large organizations to run controlled experiments on digital properties. It has no consumer-facing content hosting surface that fraudsters would target.

Optimizely Commerce.description: Optimizely Commerce is a complete suite for digital ecommerce and content management that uses artificial intelligence to deliver personalised experiences, individualised search rankings and product recommendations.
Optimizely Commerce.history: Optimizely Commerce is the B2C e-commerce product within the Optimizely digital experience platform. It originated from the Episerver Commerce module, which was folded into the Optimizely brand in 2021 after Episerver (owned by Insight Partners) acquired Optimizely and rebranded the combined company.
Optimizely Commerce.summary_abuse: Optimizely Commerce has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise e-commerce and content management platform deployed by established businesses; the platform does not expose consumer-facing hosting that scammers would leverage for phishing or fraud.

Optimizely Content Management.description: Optimizely Content Management (formerly EPiServer) is digital content, ecommerce, and marketing management solution designed for editors and marketers.
Optimizely Content Management.history: Optimizely Content Management originated as EPiServer, founded in Sweden in 1994 as one of the earliest enterprise CMS platforms. EPiServer later acquired Ektron in 2015 and merged with the Optimizely experimentation platform in 2021 under the Optimizely brand following Insight Partners' acquisition. The product is now marketed as Optimizely CMS.
Optimizely Content Management.summary_abuse: Optimizely Content Management has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise CMS deployed by established organizations and does not expose consumer-facing hosting that fraudsters would exploit.

Optimole.description: Optimole is a cloud-based platform that provides media optimization, real-time image transformations, and digital asset management for improved website performance and delivery.
Optimole.history: Optimole was launched around 2018 by Themeisle, a Romanian WordPress theme and plugin company. It provides cloud-based image optimization and delivery for WordPress sites. No notable ownership changes beyond the parent company relationship are documented.
Optimole.summary_abuse: Optimole has no notable abuse signal. It is an image optimization CDN for WordPress sites with no consumer-facing content hosting that fraudsters would target.

OptiMonk.description: OptiMonk is an on-site message toolkit used to improve conversions using action-based popups ad bars.
OptiMonk.history: OptiMonk was founded in 2014 in Hungary as a conversion optimization and exit-intent popup tool. It has grown into a mainstream on-site messaging platform used by e-commerce businesses worldwide. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.

No documented incidents of the platform being used for phishing or fraud.

Optimove.description: Optimove is a relationship marketing hub powered by a combination of advanced customer modeling, predictive micro-segmentation and campaign automation technologies.

Optimove.summary_abuse: Optimove has no notable abuse signal. Optimy.description: Optimy is an all-in-one CSR platform that enhances social and business impact by streamlining social programs.

OptinMonster.description: OptinMonster is a conversion optimisation tool that allows you to grow your email list.

Oracle Application Express.description: Oracle Application Express (APEX) is an enterprise low-code development platform from Oracle Corporation. APEX is a fully supported no-cost feature of Oracle Database.
Oracle Application Express.history: Oracle APEX (Application Express) was originally called HTML DB and first released in 2004 as an enterprise low-code development platform built into the Oracle Database. Oracle Application Server.history: Oracle Application Server (OAS, also known as Oracle iAS) was Oracle's J2EE application server product, first released around 2000 as Oracle's response to IBM WebSphere and Sun's Java EE stack. Oracle has since deprecated OAS in favor of Oracle WebLogic Server following the acquisition of BEA Systems in 2008.
Oracle Application Server.summary_abuse: Oracle Application Server has no notable abuse signal. It is a deprecated enterprise application server product deployed within organizational data centers, with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Oracle BlueKai.description: Oracle BlueKai is a cloud-based big data platform that enables companies to personalise online, offline, and mobile marketing campaigns.
Oracle BlueKai.history: BlueKai was founded in 2007 as an independent data management platform (DMP) and advertising data marketplace. Oracle acquired BlueKai in 2014 for approximately $400 million, rebranding it as Oracle Data Cloud and later Oracle BlueKai. In 2020, a security incident exposed a database containing billions of records of personal consumer data collected through its tracking infrastructure.
Oracle BlueKai.summary_abuse: Oracle BlueKai is a data management platform that tracks user behavior across the web for advertising targeting. In 2020, a major security incident exposed billions of personal data records from its tracking database due to an unsecured server. As a data broker and ad-tech platform, it collects detailed behavioral profiles on consumers without direct opt-in, raising significant privacy concerns. It is not typically used by external threat actors for fraud but is itself a subject of privacy criticism.

Oracle Commerce.description: Oracle Commerce is a unified B2B and B2C ecommerce platform.
Oracle Commerce.history: Oracle Commerce originated as ATG (Art Technology Group) Web Commerce, founded in 1991 and publicly traded before Oracle acquired ATG in 2011. Oracle integrated the platform into its Customer Experience suite, merging it with Endeca (also acquired in 2011) to create Oracle Commerce, which supports unified B2B and B2C e-commerce scenarios.
Oracle Commerce.summary_abuse: Oracle Commerce has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise e-commerce platform deployed by established businesses on their own infrastructure or Oracle cloud, with no anonymous consumer-facing content hosting that fraudsters would exploit.

Oracle Commerce Cloud.history: Oracle Commerce Cloud is the cloud-native SaaS version of Oracle's e-commerce offering, launched around 2015 as part of Oracle Cloud. It is built on the foundation of the Oracle Commerce platform (originally ATG) and delivered as a fully managed SaaS service supporting B2C and B2B scenarios on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.
Oracle Commerce Cloud.summary_abuse: Oracle Commerce Cloud has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise e-commerce SaaS platform deployed by established retailers and B2B organizations, without anonymous consumer-facing content hosting that fraudsters would exploit.

Oracle Content Management.description: Oracle Content Management is a cloud-based platform designed for organisations to create, store, manage, and deliver digital content while supporting structured content organisation, collaboration, and seamless integration with other applications.
Oracle Content Management.history: Oracle Content Management is a cloud-based CMS and digital asset management platform launched by Oracle around 2018 as part of Oracle Cloud. It targets enterprise organizations needing structured content management and integration with other Oracle Cloud applications.
Oracle Content Management.summary_abuse: Oracle Content Management has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise CMS deployed by organizations within Oracle Cloud, with no consumer-facing content hosting that fraudsters would exploit.

Oracle Dynamic Monitoring Service.description: Oracle Dynamic Monitoring Service is a feature of Oracle WebLogic Server that provides real-time monitoring and diagnostic capabilities for Java applications running on the WebLogic Server.
Oracle Dynamic Monitoring Service.history: Oracle Dynamic Monitoring Service (DMS) is a built-in feature of Oracle WebLogic Server that provides real-time performance metrics and diagnostics for Java EE applications. It has been part of the WebLogic platform since at least the early 2010s.
Oracle Dynamic Monitoring Service.summary_abuse: Oracle Dynamic Monitoring Service has no abuse signal. It is an internal monitoring component of Oracle WebLogic Server used for application performance diagnostics within enterprise environments, with no consumer-facing surface.

Oracle HTTP Server.history: Oracle HTTP Server is Oracle's enterprise web server, built on the Apache HTTP Server codebase, introduced as part of Oracle Application Server in the late 1990s and later integrated into Oracle Fusion Middleware. It serves as the front-end listener for Oracle WebLogic Server deployments and has tracked Apache HTTP Server releases through the 11g and 12c product generations. No ownership changes — Oracle Corporation has always owned it.
Oracle HTTP Server.summary_abuse: Oracle HTTP Server has no notable abuse signal. It is a backend enterprise web server deployed in on-premises Oracle Fusion Middleware environments with no consumer-facing hosting surface that would attract phishing or scam operators.

Oracle Infinity.description: Oracle Infinity is a digital analytics platform for tracking, measuring, and optimizing the performance and visitor behavior of enterprise websites and mobile apps.
Oracle Infinity.history: Oracle acquired the Infinity big data analytics platform and integrated it into its Marketing Cloud as Oracle Infinity Behavioral Intelligence, rebranding and expanding it for enterprise digital analytics. It remains part of Oracle CX Marketing and is actively maintained as a SaaS offering.
Oracle Infinity.summary_abuse: Oracle Infinity is an enterprise analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B SaaS tool sold to large enterprises for tracking website visitor behavior, with no consumer-facing surface that would be targeted by phishing or fraud operators.

Oracle Maxymiser.description: Oracle Maxymiser is a real-time behavioral targeting, in-session personalisation, and product recommendations platform.
Oracle Maxymiser.history: Maxymiser was founded in 2006 as an A/B testing and multivariate optimization company originally developed in Ukraine. Oracle acquired it in September 2015 to fill a gap in its Marketing Cloud against Adobe Target and Salesforce personalization tools. Oracle has since been sunsetting Maxymiser in favor of other personalization approaches within its CX suite.
Oracle Maxymiser.summary_abuse: Oracle Maxymiser has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B personalization and A/B testing platform used by enterprise marketing teams, with no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or fraud operators.

Oracle Moat Measurement.description: Oracle Moat delivers solutions that are critical to measuring advertising effectiveness, including verification and attention, reach, and frequency as well as sales lift measurement.
Oracle Moat Measurement.history: Moat was founded in 2010 in New York as an ad measurement and attention analytics company. Oracle acquired Moat for approximately $850 million in April 2017 and integrated it into Oracle Data Cloud as Oracle Moat Measurement, giving Oracle its first dedicated digital advertising measurement capability.
Oracle Moat Measurement.summary_abuse: Oracle Moat Measurement has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise B2B advertising measurement and analytics platform used by brands, agencies, and publishers to verify ad viewability and effectiveness, with no consumer-facing surface targeted by fraud operators.

Oracle Recommendations On Demand.history: Oracle Recommendations On Demand is a cloud-based product recommendations engine that was part of the Oracle ATG Commerce platform, providing collaborative filtering and personalized suggestions for ecommerce storefronts. It has been superseded by native recommendation features within Oracle Commerce Cloud and is no longer actively marketed as a standalone product.
Oracle Recommendations On Demand.summary_abuse: Oracle Recommendations On Demand has no notable abuse signal. It is a backend ecommerce recommendation engine embedded in Oracle ATG Commerce deployments, with no consumer-facing surface that fraud operators would target.

Oracle Web Cache.description: Oracle Web Cache is a browser and content management server, which improves the performance of web sites.
Oracle Web Cache.history: Oracle Web Cache was a content-caching server included in Oracle Application Server and early Oracle Fusion Middleware releases, designed to accelerate web site performance. Oracle deprecated and removed it from Fusion Middleware 12c, with 11.1.1.9 being the final supported version.
Oracle Web Cache.summary_abuse: Oracle Web Cache has no notable abuse signal. It is a deprecated backend caching server that was deployed in Oracle enterprise middleware environments with no consumer-facing surface relevant to phishing or fraud.

Oracle WebLogic Server.description: Oracle WebLogic Server is a Java-based application server that provides a platform for developing, deploying, and running enterprise-level Java applications.
Oracle WebLogic Server.history: Oracle WebLogic Server originated from BEA Systems, which launched WebLogic as one of the first Java application servers in the mid-1990s. BEA WebLogic became the dominant Java EE application server for enterprises before Oracle acquired BEA Systems for $8.5 billion in 2008, rebranding it as Oracle WebLogic Server and integrating it as the core runtime of Oracle Fusion Middleware.
Oracle WebLogic Server.summary_abuse: Oracle WebLogic Server has no notable consumer-facing abuse signal; however, it is a historically high-value target for exploitation. Multiple critical deserialization and remote code execution vulnerabilities (CVE-2017-10271, CVE-2019-2725, and others) have been actively exploited by ransomware groups and cryptocurrency mining malware to compromise enterprise servers. Oracle patches these actively and enterprises are responsible for timely patching.

Orama.description: Orama is a platform offering full-text and vector search capabilities across 300 global locations.
Orama.history: Orama was initially released as Lyra, an open-source full-text search engine written in TypeScript, around 2022. The project was renamed Orama and expanded to include vector search, hybrid search, and a cloud hosting platform (Orama Cloud) delivered across a global edge network. The open-source core remains Apache-2.0 licensed with an active community.
Orama.summary_abuse: Orama has no notable abuse signal. It is an open-source search engine library and associated cloud platform used by developers to add search capabilities to applications, with no consumer-facing hosting surface that would attract phishing or fraud operators.

Orankl.description: Orankl is a provider email marketing and review services.
Orankl.history: Orankl is a small San Francisco-based SaaS company offering email marketing automation and customer review collection for ecommerce merchants. It has bootstrapped to over 1,000 customers as of 2021 with no disclosed external funding.
Orankl.summary_abuse: Orankl has no notable abuse signal. It is a small ecommerce email marketing and reviews platform used by small online retailers, with no consumer-facing hosting surface that fraud operators would specifically target.

Orbit Slider.history: Orbit is a jQuery image slider built by ZURB as a playground project, released around 2010. It was widely used during the era of jQuery-based carousels but has since been deprecated as modern CSS and JavaScript alternatives replaced it.

Orchard Core.description: Orchard Core is an open-source modular and multi-tenant application framework built with ASP.NET Core, and a content management system (CMS).

Ortto.history: Ortto was originally founded in Australia as Autopilot, a visual marketing automation platform, around 2012-2015. The company rebranded to Ortto in 2021 when it expanded its product to include a customer data platform and analytics alongside its existing journey-based marketing automation tools.
Ortto.summary_abuse: Ortto has no notable abuse signal. It is a marketing automation and customer data platform used by legitimate SaaS and ecommerce businesses, with no documented use in phishing or spam campaigns beyond what is typical of email marketing platforms.

Osano.description: Osano is a data privacy platform that helps your website become compliant with laws such as GDPR and CCPA.
Osano.history: Osano was founded in 2018 in Austin, Texas with backing from LiveOak Ventures. It launched its data privacy and compliance platform publicly in October 2019 to help companies comply with GDPR, CCPA, and other global privacy laws. The company has expanded to include cookie consent management, data mapping, vendor risk management, and subject rights request workflows.
Osano.summary_abuse: Osano has no notable abuse signal. It is a data privacy compliance platform built specifically to reduce privacy law violations and manage consent, making it an inherently privacy-protective tool rather than a vehicle for abuse.

osCommerce.description: OsCommerce is an open-source ecommerce and online store-management software program.
osCommerce.history: osCommerce was created by Harald Ponce de Leon in 2000 as one of the earliest open-source ecommerce platforms. It had widespread adoption in the early 2000s but lost ground to WooCommerce, Magento, and Shopify. The platform has been largely dormant in recent years, with many legacy installations still running but no significant new development.
osCommerce.summary_abuse: osCommerce installations are frequently targeted by attackers due to the large number of outdated, unpatched deployments. Known vulnerabilities in old versions have been exploited for card skimming, malware injection, and fake storefront setup. The platform itself is benign software but legacy deployments are a common target for web skimmers.

OSI Tracker.description: OSI Tracker is an affiliate marketing and tracking software.
OSI Tracker.history: OSI Tracker is an affiliate marketing and tracking SaaS platform. No notable founding year or corporate history is publicly documented.
OSI Tracker.summary_abuse: OSI Tracker is an affiliate marketing tracking platform. Affiliate marketing tools are occasionally exploited to manage fraudulent affiliate schemes or cookie stuffing. No specific documented abuse incidents for OSI Tracker are known, but the category carries moderate inherent risk.

Osterreichische Post.description: Österreichische Post is an Austrian logistics and postal services provider.
Osterreichische Post.history: Osterreichische Post AG is Austria's national postal service provider, incorporated as a public company and listed on the Vienna Stock Exchange. Its shipping carrier integration appears in ecommerce platforms for Austrian parcel delivery and tracking services.
Osterreichische Post.summary_abuse: Osterreichische Post has no notable abuse signal as a technology. It is the Austrian national postal carrier integrated into ecommerce shipping workflows, with no consumer-facing hosting surface that fraud operators would specifically target.

osTicket.description: osTicket is an open-source support ticket system that allows businesses to manage and streamline customer support requests through a centralized platform, including features for ticket tracking, automation, and reporting.
osTicket.history: osTicket is an open-source support ticket system created around 2003. It is one of the most widely deployed self-hosted helpdesk platforms and is maintained by Enhancesoft, which offers a cloud-hosted version alongside the free self-hosted version.
osTicket.summary_abuse: osTicket is a helpdesk platform with no notable abuse signal related to phishing or fraud. It is occasionally targeted by attackers seeking to exploit vulnerabilities in unpatched installations, but this is typical for any widely deployed web application.

ostr.io.description: ostr.io is a platform that provides site monitoring and analytics services, enabling the collection and analysis of performance and usage data.
ostr.io.history: ostr.io is a web services platform founded in 2015 by VeliOV Group, offering uptime monitoring, SEO pre-rendering, web analytics, and security features. It targets JavaScript developers and small web teams.
ostr.io.summary_abuse: ostr.io is a developer-focused monitoring and analytics platform with no notable abuse signal.

otrs.history: OTRS (Open Ticket Request System) was created in 2001 in Germany as an open-source help desk and IT service management platform. OTRS GmbH was formally founded in 2003. The software has a community edition (OTRS Free) and a commercial version. It is widely used in IT service management, particularly in Europe.
otrs.summary_abuse: OTRS is a help desk and ITSM platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by organizations for internal ticket management and customer support, with no consumer-facing hosting features that would attract fraudsters.

Otter Blocks.description: Otter blocks is a Gutenberg Blocks page builder plugin that adds extra functionality to the WordPress Block Editor.
Otter Blocks.history: Otter Blocks is a free WordPress Gutenberg blocks plugin developed by Themeisle, a Romanian WordPress theme and plugin company. It provides additional block types and patterns for the WordPress Block Editor and is actively maintained alongside Themeisle's premium product lineup.
Otter Blocks.summary_abuse: Otter Blocks has no notable abuse signal. It is a free WordPress Gutenberg plugin distributed through WordPress.org with standard plugin repository review, used by website builders to add content blocks.

OtterText.description: OtterText is a platform offering SMS marketing and automation solutions designed to enhance communication and streamline marketing campaigns.
OtterText.history: OtterText is a small SMS marketing and automation SaaS platform providing bulk messaging, automated campaigns, and subscriber management tools for businesses.
OtterText.summary_abuse: OtterText is an SMS marketing platform with mass-text capabilities. SMS marketing tools can be misused for unsolicited bulk messaging and spam campaigns; however, reputable SMS platforms enforce TCPA compliance and require subscriber consent. No specific abuse incidents involving OtterText have been documented.

Otto.description: Otto is a veterinary workflow platform that streamlines scheduling, communication, and payment processes in a single system.
Otto.history: Otto is a veterinary workflow SaaS platform providing scheduling, client communication, and payment processing tools for veterinary practices, helping clinics streamline their operations in a single system.
Otto.summary_abuse: Otto has no notable abuse signal. It is a specialized SaaS platform for veterinary practice management with a narrow vertical focus and no consumer-facing hosting surface that would attract fraud operators.

OTYS.description: OTYS is a Dutch company that specialises in providing recruitment and staffing agencies with software solutions to manage their workflows, including applicant tracking systems, job board integrations, and candidate sourcing tools.
OTYS.history: OTYS was founded in 2001 by Jorrit Blok in Veenendaal, Netherlands as a recruitment applicant tracking system. The company grew to serve over 1,000 customers with 15,000+ recruiters across 25 European countries before being acquired by Mysolution to strengthen that company's recruitment software portfolio.
OTYS.summary_abuse: OTYS has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B recruitment platform used by staffing agencies with no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Outbrain.description: Outbrain is a web advertising platform that displays boxes of links, known as chumboxes, to pages within websites.
Outbrain.history: Outbrain was founded in 2006 in New York as a content recommendation and native advertising network. It grew into one of the largest open-web recommendation platforms (commonly called a chumbox network) serving major publisher sites. Outbrain merged with Teads in 2024 to form a larger advertising group.
Outbrain.summary_abuse: Outbrain is a native advertising and content recommendation network that has historically been associated with low-quality clickbait content, misleading ad formats, and occasionally malicious ads (malvertising). The chumbox format is widely criticized for promoting sensational and deceptive content. Outbrain's ad review process has been publicly criticized for permitting misleading health and financial product ads. The heavy tracker density reflects its core ad-tech business model of cross-site user profiling.

Outfindo.description: Outfindo is a platform that provides product selection guides for websites to improve customer shopping experiences and boost conversions.
Outfindo.history: Outfindo is a small SaaS platform providing product selection guides and shopping advisors for ecommerce websites to help customers navigate product choices and improve conversion rates.
Outfindo.summary_abuse: Outfindo has no notable abuse signal. It is a product selection guide widget for ecommerce stores with no independent consumer-facing surface that fraud operators would target.

Outfunnel.description: Outfunnel is a platform that connects sales and marketing data to streamline workflows and improve data alignment across tools.
Outfunnel.history: Outfunnel is an Estonian SaaS company founded around 2018 that connects CRM and marketing tools to align sales and marketing data workflows, integrating platforms like HubSpot, Pipedrive, and Mailchimp.
Outfunnel.summary_abuse: Outfunnel has no notable abuse signal. It is a data integration tool connecting CRM and marketing platforms used by legitimate B2B sales teams, with no consumer-facing hosting surface that would attract fraud operators.

Outlook Web App.description: Outlook on the web is an information manager web app. It includes a web-based email client, a calendar tool, a contact manager, and a task manager.
Outlook Web App.history: Outlook on the Web (originally called Outlook Web Access) is Microsoft's browser-based email and calendar client that ships with Exchange Server and Microsoft 365. It has been available since the late 1990s and is continuously updated as part of the Microsoft 365 ecosystem.
Outlook Web App.summary_abuse: Outlook Web App is a target for credential phishing campaigns — attackers frequently create fake OWA login pages to harvest Microsoft 365 credentials from enterprise users. The legitimate service itself is not abusive, but its brand and login UI are among the most impersonated in phishing kits globally. Microsoft actively pursues phishing site takedowns and operates extensive anti-phishing infrastructure within Microsoft 365.

Outplay.description: Outplay is a sales engagement platform that uses AI to optimize interactions and support fast-scaling small and medium-sized businesses.
Outplay.history: Outplay is an AI-powered sales engagement platform founded around 2019, targeting small and medium-sized sales teams with multi-channel outreach automation including email, phone, and LinkedIn sequences.
Outplay.summary_abuse: Outplay has no notable abuse signal. It is a sales engagement platform for legitimate B2B sales teams, though like all multi-channel outreach tools it can be misused for spam if abused by bad actors. No specific incidents have been documented.

OutSystems.description: OutSystems is a low-code platform which provides tools for companies to develop, deploy and manage omnichannel enterprise applications.
OutSystems.history: OutSystems was founded in 2001 in Lisbon, Portugal and pioneered the low-code enterprise application development platform category. The company has raised over $1 billion in private equity funding and serves thousands of enterprise customers globally, including many Fortune 500 companies. It remains privately held with a Lisbon HQ and global expansion.
OutSystems.summary_abuse: OutSystems has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise low-code development platform used by large organizations to build internal applications, with no consumer-facing hosting surface that would attract phishing or fraud operators.

OutTheBoxThemes Panoramic.description: Panoramic is a fully responsive WordPress theme with a homepage slider by OutTheBoxThemes.
OutTheBoxThemes Panoramic.history: Panoramic is a free WordPress theme created by OutTheBoxThemes, a small UK WordPress theme developer. It is distributed on WordPress.org with a premium upgrade option.
OutTheBoxThemes Panoramic.summary_abuse: Panoramic is a WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. WordPress themes themselves have no consumer-facing hosted surface exploitable by fraudsters.

Ova.description: Ova is a website builder platform from Australia.
Ova.history: Ova is an Australian website builder platform serving small businesses with drag-and-drop website creation tools and hosting.
Ova.summary_abuse: Ova has no notable documented abuse signal. It is a small Australian website builder; website builders with free or low-cost tiers can be used to host phishing pages, but no specific incidents involving Ova have been documented.

Overblog.description: Overblog is a French blog platform that enables users to create and share content.
Overblog.history: Overblog is a French consumer blogging platform founded around 2004, providing free blog creation and hosting. It was one of the early major French-language blogging platforms and has been acquired multiple times, operating under various parent companies over the years.
Overblog.summary_abuse: Overblog is a free consumer blogging platform that, like most open hosting platforms with free tiers, has some exposure to spam and low-quality content being published on its subdomain infrastructure. The free tier with content hosting creates some risk of misuse for phishing or SEO spam, though no major documented abuse campaigns are associated specifically with Overblog.

Overfull.description: Overfull is a reservation management system designed for restaurants to handle bookings, manage tables, and streamline guest scheduling.
Overfull.history: Overfull is a French restaurant reservation management system providing table booking and guest scheduling tools for restaurant operators.
Overfull.summary_abuse: Overfull has no notable abuse signal. It is a restaurant reservation management system with no consumer-facing hosting surface that fraud operators would target.

Overheat.description: Overheat is a conversion optimization package designed to improve website performance by enhancing user experience and increasing conversion rates through data-driven strategies and tailored solutions.
Overheat.history: Overheat is an Italian conversion optimization SaaS platform offering A/B testing and user experience improvement tools for websites.
Overheat.summary_abuse: Overheat has no notable abuse signal. It is a conversion optimization and A/B testing tool for website operators with no consumer-facing hosting surface that fraud operators would target.

Overloop.description: Overloop is an AI-powered sales prospecting platform that automates lead generation, outreach, and engagement to optimize customer acquisition and conversion.
Overloop.history: Overloop is a Belgian AI-powered sales prospecting and outreach automation platform founded around 2016, providing lead generation and multi-channel engagement tools for sales teams.
Overloop.summary_abuse: Overloop has no notable abuse signal beyond the inherent risk of any sales outreach automation platform. Like similar tools, it could theoretically be misused for spam prospecting, but no specific documented incidents exist and legitimate use dominates.

Overwolf.description: Overwolf is a platform that enables the creation, sharing, and monetization of in-game apps and mods.
Overwolf.history: Overwolf was founded in 2012 in Israel as a platform enabling developers to create, distribute, and monetize in-game overlays and mods. It has raised substantial VC funding and grown into the dominant platform for in-game apps, acquiring CurseForge from Twitch/Amazon in 2021 to become a major gaming mod distribution hub.
Overwolf.summary_abuse: Overwolf has a moderate abuse risk profile. As an in-game overlay platform and app store, it has been a vector for potentially unwanted programs (PUPs) and adware bundled with mods or apps. The platform collects significant usage data for advertising purposes. CurseForge (now under Overwolf) experienced a notable supply chain attack in 2023 where malicious code was injected into popular Minecraft mods to deliver malware.

OVHcloud.description: OVHcloud is a global, cloud provider delivering hosted private cloud, public cloud, and dedicated server solutions.
OVHcloud.history: OVHcloud was founded in November 1999 by Octave Klaba in Roubaix, France, as a web hosting provider. It grew to become Europe's largest cloud provider. In October 2021, OVHcloud listed on Euronext Paris, raising €400M with an opening market capitalisation of €3.48B. In March 2021, a major fire at the Strasbourg data center destroyed infrastructure and caused significant service disruptions.
OVHcloud.summary_abuse: OVHcloud has moderate abuse signal common to large cloud infrastructure providers. Its dedicated servers and VPS hosting have historically been used for botnets, spam operations, and phishing infrastructure, particularly due to competitive pricing. OVHcloud publishes an AUP and has an abuse team, but its response speed has been criticized compared to hyperscalers. It is not disproportionately favored by fraudsters relative to its market size.

Owids.description: Owids is a set of widgets designed to improve website conversion rates and visitor engagement across platforms without requiring coding skills.
Owids.history: Owids is a small SaaS widget platform for conversion optimization, launched around 2020.
Owids.summary_abuse: Owids is a small conversion widget tool with no notable abuse signal.

OWL Carousel.description: OWL Carousel is an enabled jQuery plugin that lets you create responsive carousel sliders.
OWL Carousel.history: OWL Carousel is a jQuery-based responsive carousel plugin originally created around 2013. OWL Carousel 2 was released as a rewrite and the last major update was in 2017. The project has been largely abandoned since then but remains widely embedded on older websites.
OWL Carousel.summary_abuse: OWL Carousel has no abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript carousel library with no consumer-facing surface.

ownCloud.history: ownCloud was founded in 2010 by Frank Karlitschek as an open-source file sync and share platform. In 2016, Karlitschek left to create Nextcloud as a fork, dividing the community. ownCloud GmbH continued development, though Nextcloud has since become more widely adopted.
html:
ownCloud Inc\.
Your Cloud, Your Data, Your Way!
ownCloud.summary_abuse: ownCloud is a self-hosted file sync and share platform. Exposed or misconfigured instances have occasionally been targeted by attackers, but this is not specific to ownCloud's design. The software itself has no features that would directly facilitate fraud or phishing.

Ownpage.description: Ownpage is a technology services provider that enables publishers to personalize their newsletters, websites, and applications, enhancing content delivery for media platforms.
Ownpage.history: Ownpage is a French B2B SaaS platform enabling media publishers to personalize newsletters and digital content through audience segmentation and content delivery tools.
Ownpage.summary_abuse: Ownpage has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B personalization platform for media publishers with no consumer-facing hosting surface that fraud operators would target.

Oxatis.description: Oxatis is a cloud-based ecommerce solution which enables users to create and manage their own online store websites.
Oxatis.history: Oxatis is a French cloud-based ecommerce platform founded around 2001, providing small and medium businesses with tools to create and manage online stores. It primarily serves the French-speaking European market.
Oxatis.summary_abuse: Oxatis has no notable abuse signal. It is a French ecommerce platform used by legitimate SMB retailers; like all ecommerce platforms it can theoretically host fake storefronts, but no documented abuse campaigns specifically targeting Oxatis infrastructure have been reported.

Oxi Social Login.description: Oxi Social Login provides one click login with services like Facebook, Google and many more.
Oxi Social Login.history: Oxi Social Login is a social login plugin/widget from OxiApps, a small Indian software developer, offering one-click OAuth login via Facebook, Google, and other providers.
Oxi Social Login.summary_abuse: Oxi Social Login is a social OAuth integration tool. Social login widgets that pass OAuth tokens can present privacy risks if poorly implemented, but there are no documented specific abuse incidents for this vendor.

OXID eShop.description: OXID eShop is a free, open source ecommerce solution built using object oriented programming and PHP.
OXID eShop.history: OXID eSales AG was founded in 2003 by Roland Fesenmayr in Freiburg im Breisgau, Germany. OXID eShop launched in 2005 as a PHP-based open-source ecommerce platform. It has been a leading German ecommerce solution for B2B and B2C use cases for over 20 years.
OXID eShop.summary_abuse: OXID eShop has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted open-source ecommerce platform primarily used by legitimate German businesses, with no centralized hosting surface targeted by fraudsters.

OXID eShop Community Edition.description: OXID eShop Community Edition is a free, open source ecommerce solution built using object oriented programming and PHP.
OXID eShop Community Edition.history: OXID eShop Community Edition is the free, GPL-licensed tier of the OXID eShop platform developed by OXID eSales AG (founded 2003, Freiburg, Germany). It provides the same core PHP ecommerce functionality as the paid editions without enterprise support.
OXID eShop Community Edition.summary_abuse: OXID eShop Community Edition has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted open-source ecommerce platform used by legitimate German businesses.

OXID eShop Enterprise Edition.description: OXID eShop Enterprise Edition is a B2B or B2C ecommerce solution built using object oriented programming and PHP.
OXID eShop Enterprise Edition.history: OXID eShop Enterprise Edition is the top-tier commercial offering from OXID eSales AG, founded in 2003 in Freiburg, Germany, designed for large B2B and B2C deployments requiring advanced scalability and enterprise support.
OXID eShop Enterprise Edition.summary_abuse: OXID eShop Enterprise Edition has no notable abuse signal. It is a licensed enterprise ecommerce platform used by large German businesses.

OXID eShop Professional Edition.description: OXID eShop Professional Edition is an e-ommerce platform for both small start-up companies and experience online retailers with a wide range of functions, software maintenance and support.
OXID eShop Professional Edition.history: OXID eShop Professional Edition is the mid-tier commercial offering from OXID eSales AG, founded in 2003 in Freiburg, Germany, targeting small to midsize online retailers with enhanced functionality and software support.
OXID eShop Professional Edition.summary_abuse: OXID eShop Professional Edition has no notable abuse signal. It is a commercial ecommerce platform sold to legitimate businesses.

Oxygen.description: Oxygen Builder is a tool to build a WordPress website.
Oxygen.history: Oxygen Builder is a premium WordPress page builder plugin launched around 2016, positioning itself as a developer-focused alternative to Elementor and Divi. It uses a visual design approach without relying on shortcodes, giving developers more control over output HTML.
Oxygen.summary_abuse: Oxygen Builder has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted WordPress page builder plugin used by web developers, with no consumer-facing hosting surface that fraud operators would target.

oXyShop.description: oXyShop is a platform for creating and managing online stores.
oXyShop.history: oXyShop is a Czech e-commerce platform for creating and managing online stores; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events are documented.
oXyShop.summary_abuse: oXyShop is a Czech e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal.
